description: This document verifies that EBS volumes on the launched instance are accessible, identical to the source, and properly mounted on the EC2 instance.

Volumes.description: (Required) List of all volumes to verify (for example: [{"deviceName": "/dev/xvda", "totalStorageBytes": 8589934592}])

runCommand:
#!/bin/bash
# Backward compatibility: Use environment variable if available, otherwise fall back to parameter substitution
if [[ -z "${SSM_Volumes}" ]]; then
  export SSM_Volumes="{{Volumes}}"
fi

GB=$((1024 * 1024 * 1024))

function parse_volumes_json () {
  pyexec=python3
  which python3 > /dev/null 2>&1
  is_py3=$?
  if [ $is_py3 -eq 1 ]; then pyexec=python; fi
  $pyexec - <<END
import json
data = json.loads('${SSM_Volumes}')
for d in data:
  print(d['deviceName'] + '-' + str(d['totalStorageBytes']))
END
}

function normalize_size () {
  echo $(awk -v size_bytes="$1" -v GB="$GB" 'BEGIN {
    res = size_bytes / GB
    if (res % 1 != 0) {
      res += 0.5
    }
    printf "%.0f", res
  }')
}

user_disks=($(parse_volumes_json))

if [ "$?" -ne "0" ]; then
  echo "Error: failed to parse Volumes"
  exit 1
fi

readarray -t lsblk_disks_output_arr < <(lsblk -d -e 7 --noheadings -b -o NAME,SIZE)
system_disks_count="${#lsblk_disks_output_arr[@]}"
user_disks_count="${#user_disks[@]}"

# FSx ONTAP multipath: lsblk -d counts raw paths (2 per LUN), adjust to mapper count (= actual LUNs)
mp_dev_list=$(ls /dev/mapper/3* /dev/mapper/mpath* 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "$mp_dev_list" ] && [ -f "/var/log/mgn_iscsi_postboot.log" ]; then
  iscsi_raw_count=$(lsblk -ndo NAME,TRAN 2>/dev/null | grep -c iscsi || true)
  mp_lun_count=$(echo "$mp_dev_list" | wc -l)
  system_disks_count=$(( system_disks_count - iscsi_raw_count + mp_lun_count ))
  echo "Note: adjusted disk count for multipath ($iscsi_raw_count iSCSI paths -> $mp_lun_count LUNs)"
fi

if [ "$system_disks_count" -ne "$user_disks_count" ]; then
  echo "Error: found #$system_disks_count disks, expected #$user_disks_count"
  exit 1
fi

for i in "${!user_disks[@]}"; do
  IFS="-" read -r name sizeBytes <<< "${user_disks[$i]}"
  sizeGb=$(normalize_size "$sizeBytes")
  user_disks[$i]="$name-$sizeGb"
done

declare -a system_disks

for disk in "${lsblk_disks_output_arr[@]}"; do
  IFS=" " read -r name sizeBytes <<< "$disk"
  sizeGb=$(normalize_size "$sizeBytes")
  system_disks+=("$name-$sizeGb")
done

ret=0

for user_disk in "${user_disks[@]}"; do
  IFS="-" read -r user_disk_name user_disk_size <<< "$user_disk"
  found=0
  for i in "${!system_disks[@]}"; do

    IFS="-" read -r system_disk_name system_disk_size <<< "${system_disks[$i]}"
    if [ "$user_disk_size" == "$system_disk_size" ]; then
      echo "Success: disk $user_disk_name is found"
      unset 'system_disks[$i]'
      found=1
      break
    fi
  done

  if [ "$found" -eq "0" ]; then
    echo "Error: disk $user_disk_name is not found"
    ret=1
  fi
done

ebs_ret=$ret

# ============================================
# FSx ONTAP iSCSI/Multipath Validation
# Runs after EBS checks. Skips if no FSx ONTAP postboot log found.
# ============================================

ISCSI_POSTBOOT_LOG="/var/log/mgn_iscsi_postboot.log"
ISCSI_PREFIX="[FSx-ONTAP-Validation]"
ISCSI_WARNINGS=0
ISCSI_FAILS=0
FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT=$((user_disks_count - 1))

function iscsi_check_result () {
  local name="$1" status="$2" message="$3"
  echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX $name: $status - $message"
  [ "$status" = "WARNING" ] && ISCSI_WARNINGS=$((ISCSI_WARNINGS + 1))
  [ "$status" = "FAIL" ] && ISCSI_FAILS=$((ISCSI_FAILS + 1))
}

function get_iscsi_session_count () {
  command -v iscsiadm >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 0; return; }
  iscsiadm -m session 2>/dev/null | grep -c '^tcp:' || true
}

# Fallback for RHEL 6/CentOS 6 where lsblk TRAN column is unavailable (util-linux < 2.22)
function get_iscsi_block_devices () {
  command -v iscsiadm >/dev/null 2>&1 || return
  iscsiadm -m session -P 3 2>/dev/null | grep "Attached scsi disk" | awk '{print $4}'
}

function detect_fsx_ontap () {
  if [ -f "$ISCSI_POSTBOOT_LOG" ]; then
    echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX Detected FSx ONTAP postboot log: $ISCSI_POSTBOOT_LOG"
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

function check_multipath_service () {
  if systemctl is-active multipathd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_service" "PASS" "multipathd is active"
  elif service multipathd status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_service" "PASS" "multipathd is running (sysvinit)"
  else
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_service" "WARNING" "multipathd is not active"
  fi
}

function check_multipath_health () {
  if ! command -v multipath >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_health" "WARNING" "multipath command not found"
    return
  fi
  local mp_output
  mp_output=$(multipath -ll 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -z "$mp_output" ]; then
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_health" "WARNING" "No multipath devices found (multipath -ll returned empty)"
    return
  fi
  local unhealthy
  unhealthy=$(echo "$mp_output" | grep -iE 'faulty|offline|shaky' || true)
  if [ -n "$unhealthy" ]; then
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_health" "WARNING" "Paths with issues: $unhealthy"
  else
    iscsi_check_result "multipath_health" "PASS" "All multipath paths healthy"
  fi
}

function check_iscsi_device_count () {
  local iscsi_count
  local mp_device_count
  mp_device_count=$(ls /dev/mapper/3* /dev/mapper/mpath* 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  if [ "$mp_device_count" -gt 0 ]; then
    # Multipath active: verify LUN count and paths per LUN
    iscsi_count=$mp_device_count
    if [ "$iscsi_count" -ne "$FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT" ]; then
      local session_count
      session_count=$(get_iscsi_session_count)
      iscsi_check_result "device_count" "FAIL" "Found $iscsi_count multipath LUNs, expected $FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT (active iSCSI sessions: $session_count)"
      return
    fi
    # Verify each LUN has exactly 2 paths
    local bad_luns=""
    local dev
    for dev in $(ls /dev/mapper/3* /dev/mapper/mpath* 2>/dev/null); do
      local mapname
      mapname=$(basename "$dev")
      local path_count
      path_count=$(multipath -ll "$mapname" 2>/dev/null | grep -cE '[0-9]+:[0-9]+:[0-9]+:[0-9]+' || true)
      if [ "$path_count" -ne 2 ]; then
        bad_luns="$bad_luns $mapname($path_count paths)"
      fi
    done
    if [ -n "$bad_luns" ]; then
      iscsi_check_result "device_count" "FAIL" "LUNs without 2 paths:$bad_luns"
    else
      iscsi_check_result "device_count" "PASS" "Found $iscsi_count multipath LUNs, each with 2 paths (expected $FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT)"
    fi
  else
    # No multipath: count raw iSCSI block devices
    iscsi_count=$(lsblk -ndo NAME,TRAN 2>/dev/null | grep -c iscsi || true)
    if [ "$iscsi_count" -eq 0 ]; then
      iscsi_count=$(get_iscsi_block_devices | wc -l)
    fi
    local session_count
    session_count=$(get_iscsi_session_count)
    if [ "$iscsi_count" -eq "$FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT" ]; then
      iscsi_check_result "device_count" "PASS" "Found $iscsi_count iSCSI devices (expected $FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT)"
    elif [ "$iscsi_count" -gt 0 ]; then
      iscsi_check_result "device_count" "FAIL" "Found $iscsi_count iSCSI devices, expected exactly $FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT (active iSCSI sessions: $session_count)"
    else
      iscsi_check_result "device_count" "FAIL" "No iSCSI devices found (expected $FSX_ONTAP_EXPECTED_COUNT, active iSCSI sessions: $session_count)"
    fi
  fi
}

function check_iscsi_volume_mounts () {
  local mp_device_count
  mp_device_count=$(ls /dev/mapper/3* /dev/mapper/mpath* 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  local mp_mounts
  mp_mounts=$(mount | grep -cE '/dev/mapper/(3|mpath)' || true)
  if [ "$mp_mounts" -gt 0 ]; then
    if [ "$mp_device_count" -gt 0 ] && [ "$mp_mounts" -lt "$mp_device_count" ]; then
      iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "FAIL" "Only $mp_mounts of $mp_device_count multipath devices mounted. Check /etc/fstab entries and filesystem state"
    else
      iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "PASS" "$mp_mounts volumes mounted via multipath devices"
    fi
    return
  fi
  local iscsi_devs
  iscsi_devs=$(lsblk -ndo NAME,TRAN 2>/dev/null | awk '/iscsi/{print $1}')
  if [ -z "$iscsi_devs" ]; then
    iscsi_devs=$(get_iscsi_block_devices)
  fi
  local iscsi_dev_count
  iscsi_dev_count=$(echo "$iscsi_devs" | grep -c . || true)
  local sd_iscsi_mounts=0
  local dev
  for dev in $iscsi_devs; do
    mount | grep -qE "^/dev/${dev}[0-9]* " && sd_iscsi_mounts=$((sd_iscsi_mounts + 1))
  done
  if [ "$sd_iscsi_mounts" -gt 0 ]; then
    if [ "$sd_iscsi_mounts" -lt "$iscsi_dev_count" ]; then
      iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "FAIL" "Only $sd_iscsi_mounts of $iscsi_dev_count iSCSI devices mounted (no multipath). Check /etc/fstab entries and filesystem state"
    else
      local session_count
      session_count=$(get_iscsi_session_count)
      if [ "$session_count" -gt 1 ]; then
        iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "FAIL" "Multiple iSCSI paths ($iscsi_dev_count devices, $session_count sessions) without multipath - same LUNs visible as duplicate block devices. Data corruption risk"
      else
        iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "WARNING" "$sd_iscsi_mounts volumes mounted via single iSCSI path (no multipath redundancy)"
      fi
    fi
    return
  fi
  local session_count
  session_count=$(get_iscsi_session_count)
  if [ "$session_count" -gt 0 ]; then
    iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "FAIL" "iSCSI sessions active ($session_count) but no volumes mounted. Devices may not be partitioned or filesystems not in /etc/fstab"
  else
    iscsi_check_result "volume_mounts" "FAIL" "No iSCSI sessions or multipath mounts found. Check iSCSI target connectivity (port 3260) and security groups"
  fi
}

# Run FSx ONTAP validation if indicators present
if detect_fsx_ontap; then
  echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX === FSx ONTAP iSCSI/Multipath Validation Started ==="
  check_multipath_service
  check_multipath_health
  check_iscsi_device_count
  check_iscsi_volume_mounts

  if [ "$ISCSI_FAILS" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX overall: FAIL ($ISCSI_FAILS failures, $ISCSI_WARNINGS warnings)"
    echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX Troubleshooting guide: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/fsx-ontap-troubleshooting.html#fsx-iscsi-troubleshooting"
    exit 1
  elif [ "$ISCSI_WARNINGS" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX overall: WARNING ($ISCSI_WARNINGS warnings)"
    echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX Troubleshooting guide: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/fsx-ontap-troubleshooting.html#fsx-iscsi-troubleshooting"
  else
    echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX overall: PASS"
  fi
else
  echo "$ISCSI_PREFIX No FSx ONTAP indicators found - skipping iSCSI validation"
fi

exit $ebs_ret
